key_points: The cell is the basic structural and functional unit of life
Cells were discovered by Robert Hooke in 1665 while observing cork under a microscope
Cell theory states that all living organisms are made of cells and cells arise from pre-existing cells
The plasma membrane surrounds the cell and controls the movement of substances in and out
The cell wall is present in plant cells and provides protection and support
The nucleus controls cell activities and contains genetic material
Cytoplasm is the jelly-like substance where most metabolic reactions occur
Mitochondria produce energy in the form of ATP and are called the powerhouse of the cell
Ribosomes are responsible for protein synthesis in the cell
Cell division helps in growth, repair and reproduction
formulas: Diffusion → Movement of molecules from higher concentration to lower concentration
Osmosis → Movement of water through a selectively permeable membrane
Mitosis → One cell divides into two identical daughter cells
Meiosis → One cell divides into four daughter cells with half chromosome number
exam_tips: Remember that the nucleus controls the activities of the cell
Plant cells have cell wall and chloroplast but animal cells do not
Mitochondria are called the powerhouse of the cell because they produce energy
Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes and help break down wastes
Cell division is essential for growth and repair of tissues
